text:
Once upon a time in the sleepy town of Everdell, there lived a charismatic baker named Clara.

Every morning she would open her cozy bakery, fill her shelves with the sweetest treats, and lure passersby into her warm sanctuary. Clara had mastered the art of making bread so delicious that pigeons would land on her windowsills just to nibble.

One particular morning was unlike any other. Clara had a new scent she discovered: a subtle hint of forest dew mingled with a hint of wild honey. She knew this would be her big hit. Her windows were draped in blossoming roses, their petals like soft feathers.

As people wandered into her bakery to pick up their daily bread-fixes, whispers spread about the mysterious fragrance. Curiosity got the best of them, and soon there were crowds outside just to catch a whiff.

Clara stood outside during lunch hour, her hands clasped over her nose as lovely fragrance wafted through the air. Children giggled,
